message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>How helpful would a properly functioning find, test, trace, isolate and support (FTTIS) system be now in the UK with new Covid-19 infections at a low level and half the adult population immunised but with a highly transmissible variant becoming predominant?</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>a dynamic causal model of Covid-19 supplied with the latest available empirical data is used to assess the impact of a new highly transmissible variant.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Setting</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>the United Kingdom.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Participants</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>a population based study.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Interventions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>scenarios are used to explore a Covid-19 transmission rate 50% more and twice the current rate with or without a more effective FTTIS system.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main outcome measures</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>incidence, death rate and reproductive ratio</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>a small short third wave of infections occurs which does not occur if FTTIS effectiveness is improved from 25% to 30%.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>a modest improvement in FTTIS would prevent a third wave caused by a highly transmissible virus.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Modelling the effect of an improved trace and isolate system in the wake of a highly transmissible Covid-19 variant with potential vaccine escape
